jokes.text: Stop fighting with your sister! -She took my glue! -It's not yours! This is family glue!
jokes.setup: Kids fighting over glue ownership
jokes.punchline: Homer's declaration of 'family glue' as if it's communal property
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - treating glue like a family heirloom. Great economy and character voice.

jokes.text: So glue friendly, or I'll take your glue away!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to mediate the glue fight
jokes.punchline: 'Glue friendly' as a pun on 'play friendly'
jokes.explanation: Decent pun but fairly predictable wordplay. Homer's earnest delivery sells it.

jokes.text: Hey, man, I don't want your stupid glue.
jokes.setup: Lisa offers the glue to prove Bart only wants it because she's using it
jokes.punchline: Bart immediately rejects it, proving Lisa's point
jokes.explanation: Classic sibling psychology perfectly captured. Bart's character shines through.

jokes.text: -Did that make sense? -Not really, Bill. -Now I know how the Pilgrims felt. -What?
jokes.setup: Announcers realize their commentary made no sense
jokes.punchline: Even more nonsensical Pilgrim comparison
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on their own absurdity, then doubles down with an even more random reference.

jokes.text: -Who the hell is that? -Bullwinkle. -Who's that? -Underdog. Don't you know anything?
jokes.setup: Homer doesn't recognize classic cartoon characters
jokes.punchline: Bart's exasperated 'Don't you know anything?'
jokes.explanation: Great character dynamic - child schooling parent on pop culture. Bart's dismissive tone perfect.

jokes.text: They should use cartoons made in the last 50 years. This is a tradition. If you build a balloon for every cartoon character... you'll turn the parade into a farce.
jokes.setup: Bart wants newer cartoon balloons
jokes.punchline: Lisa's concern about turning the parade into a 'farce' when it already is one
jokes.explanation: Lisa's earnest defense of tradition while missing the inherent absurdity is classic character comedy.

jokes.text: -Open the can. -Where's the can opener? It's in the second drawer from the right. No, no, the other one.
jokes.setup: Homer volunteering to help with cranberry sauce
jokes.punchline: Immediate confusion over basic kitchen navigation
jokes.explanation: Standard sitcom 'husband helpless in kitchen' gag. Saved by Homer's specific characterization.

jokes.text: It's broken, Mom. Mom, it's broken. Mom, it's broken Mom, it's broken
jokes.setup: Homer struggling with the can opener
jokes.punchline: Increasingly panicked repetition like a child
jokes.explanation: Homer reverting to child-like helplessness is perfect character work. The repetition builds comedy.

jokes.text: Cranberry sauce à la Bart.
jokes.setup: Bart takes over the cranberry sauce task
jokes.punchline: Fancy French culinary terminology for opening a can
jokes.explanation: Nice contrast between pretentious presentation and simple task. Bart's cockiness shines through.

jokes.text: They're Daddy's favorite team. He wants them to lose by less than five points.
jokes.setup: Lisa explaining football to Maggie
jokes.punchline: Innocent explanation of point spread betting
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion - Lisa's earnest explanation reveals Homer's gambling in innocent terms. Perfect economy.

jokes.text: They'll feed him dinner through a tube. Hope they can fit a turkey in there.
jokes.setup: Announcer commenting on injured football player
jokes.punchline: Darkly comic Thanksgiving tie-in to serious injury
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that maintains holiday theme while being inappropriately cheerful about injury.

jokes.text: Some people find your turkey a little dry. Now they'll have an option.
jokes.setup: Patty and Selma bringing alternative food to Thanksgiving
jokes.punchline: Passive-aggressive dig at Marge's cooking disguised as helpful
jokes.explanation: Perfect Patty and Selma - undermining Marge while pretending to be helpful. Cringe-inducing family dynamics.

jokes.text: It's good to see you. Gotta pick up my old man! Bye!
jokes.setup: Homer forced to be polite to in-laws
jokes.punchline: Immediate escape with transparent excuse
jokes.explanation: Homer's social cowardice perfectly captured in his quick exit strategy.

jokes.text: -So insincere. -How does she put up with him?
jokes.setup: Homer's over-enthusiastic greeting before escaping
jokes.punchline: Sisters' immediate criticism of his transparent insincerity
jokes.explanation: Classic Patty and Selma commentary. Their unified disdain for Homer is well-established character comedy.

jokes.text: I love those kids! They got such a great attitude!
jokes.setup: Overly enthusiastic halftime show called 'Hurray for Everything'
jokes.punchline: Homer's genuine enthusiasm for obviously terrible entertainment
jokes.explanation: Homer's earnest appreciation for schlocky entertainment perfectly captures his character.

jokes.text: ...the Western hemisphere! The dancing-est hemisphere!
jokes.setup: Overly enthusiastic performance group
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical superlative about hemisphere dancing ability
jokes.explanation: Absurd patriotic cheerfulness taken to ridiculous extreme. 'Dancing-est hemisphere' is memorably silly.

jokes.text: Before we sit down to our delicious turkey purée...
jokes.setup: Thanksgiving dinner at retirement home
jokes.punchline: Calling blended turkey 'delicious' when it's clearly institutional
jokes.explanation: Dark but expected joke about nursing home food quality.

jokes.text: Oh, I knew they wouldn't forget me.
jokes.setup: Nursing home Thanksgiving announcement of relatives who called
jokes.punchline: Elderly woman's touching relief at not being forgotten
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about elderly abandonment, but delivered with genuine pathos rather than pure cruelty.

jokes.text: -This place is depressing. -Hey, I live here! Well, I'm sure it's a blast once you get used to it.
jokes.setup: Bart commenting on nursing home atmosphere
jokes.punchline: Grampa's hurt response and Bart's awkward recovery attempt
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart moment - honest to a fault, then trying to backpedal. Great family dynamics.

jokes.text: You never do anything right.
jokes.setup: Grampa's ancient mother with laryngitis saving her voice for one thing
jokes.punchline: Uses her precious words to deliver brutal criticism
jokes.explanation: Masterful setup and payoff. The economy is perfect - saving her voice for maximum cruelty is brilliantly dark.

jokes.text: A stadium's too big for flash pictures to work, but nobody cares!
jokes.setup: Describing flashbulbs going off at halftime show
jokes.punchline: Pointing out the futility while maintaining broadcast enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Great observational humor about human behavior. The 'but nobody cares' sells the absurdity.

jokes.text: That's no way to lay a fire! -This will roar any time now. -A caveman could start it.
jokes.setup: Grampa criticizing Homer's fire-making
jokes.punchline: Escalating insults about basic survival skills
jokes.explanation: Classic father-son tension. Grampa's caveman comparison is a nice touch of absurdity.

jokes.text: Holy moly! That's the biggest one of those I ever saw!
jokes.setup: Lisa's elaborate historical centerpiece
jokes.punchline: Homer's reductive response to sophisticated artwork
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer - reducing Lisa's thoughtful work to size comparison. Character-driven humor.

jokes.text: -I always said she was gifted. -Definitely from our side. Right, Mom?
jokes.setup: Parents proudly claiming credit for Lisa's talent
jokes.punchline: Asking the supremely critical mother-in-law for validation
jokes.explanation: Nice callback structure - we know asking her for validation is doomed given her earlier harshness.

jokes.text: Speaking of heroes, here's mine: Tom Turkey!
jokes.setup: Lisa's speech about unsung heroes
jokes.punchline: Homer completely missing the point, calling a turkey his hero
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer obliviousness - reduces Lisa's intellectual message to food appreciation.

jokes.text: -Yikes! What is that? -It's the centerpiece. -It's taking up real estate. -Move it, toots!
jokes.setup: Lisa's centerpiece being examined
jokes.punchline: Dismissive reactions treating art as inconvenience, Bart's casual rudeness
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of family insensitivity. 'Move it, toots!' is perfectly dismissive Bart.

jokes.text: -Hey, that got her going! -Bitchen!
jokes.setup: Lisa crying over her destroyed centerpiece
jokes.punchline: Aunts' callous enjoyment of their niece's distress
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Patty and Selma's cruel enjoyment of family drama. Dark but character-accurate.

jokes.text: I'll take some white meat to go, and send up the pie.
jokes.setup: Bart being sent to his room in disgrace
jokes.punchline: Casually ordering dinner like room service
jokes.explanation: Peak Bart - turning punishment into convenience with perfect comic timing. Excellent economy and character voice.

jokes.text: I didn't ruin Thanksgiving, she did. I always get blamed.
jokes.setup: Bart alone in his room after clearly ruining Thanksgiving
jokes.punchline: His complete inability to see his own responsibility, blaming Lisa instead
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart psychology - his complete lack of self-awareness about his role in conflicts.

jokes.text: And Lord, we're thankful for nuclear power... the cleanest, safest energy source... except for solar, which is a pipe dream.
jokes.setup: Homer leading Thanksgiving grace
jokes.punchline: Bizarre prayer topics mixing his work propaganda with dismissal of alternatives
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Homer's workplace indoctrination bleeding into religious observance. Perfect satire.

jokes.text: Not today. You saw what happened! Lord, be honest! Are we the most pathetic family in the universe?
jokes.setup: Homer continuing his prayer after the dinner disaster
jokes.punchline: Demanding divine honesty about family dysfunction in prayer
jokes.explanation: Homer's brutally honest prayer is both funny and touching. Great mix of self-awareness and desperation.

jokes.text: -Amen. -Worst prayer yet.
jokes.setup: Homer's depressing prayer ending
jokes.punchline: Grampa's matter-of-fact assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan punctuation to Homer's despair. Grampa's bluntness is expertly timed.

jokes.text: Boy, come here. You're a good dog. We don't need them to get a Thanksgiving dinner.
jokes.setup: Bart talking to Santa's Little Helper after running away
jokes.punchline: Planning to get their own Thanksgiving dinner like they're independent
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's naive confidence in his independence.

jokes.text: Smithers, every year you outstrip yourself in succulence.
jokes.setup: Burns commenting on Thanksgiving dinner
jokes.punchline: Overly formal vocabulary for simple compliment, 'succulence' sounds vaguely inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Classic Burns vocabulary - overly elaborate and slightly creepy. 'Succulence' is perfectly chosen.

jokes.text: No, I couldn't eat another bite. Dispose of all this.
jokes.setup: Burns praising the elaborate dinner
jokes.punchline: Immediately ordering it thrown away after one bite
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns moment - wasteful privilege disguised as refinement. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: The intruder is a young male, age 9 to 11. Release the hounds.
jokes.setup: Security reporting child intruder
jokes.punchline: Treating 10-year-old like dangerous criminal, ordering attack dogs
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation joke. 'Release the hounds' became a cultural reference for good reason.

jokes.text: I saw the best meals Of my generation destroyed By the madness of my brother My soul carved in slices By spiky-haired demons
jokes.setup: Lisa writing poetry about the dinner disaster
jokes.punchline: Parody of Allen Ginsberg's 'Howl' about Thanksgiving drama
jokes.explanation: Brilliant literary parody - Beat poetry applied to family dinner disaster. Shows Lisa's precocious nature perfectly.

jokes.text: Twelve bucks! Hey, I can bleed.
jokes.setup: Bart discovering blood donation pays money
jokes.punchline: Casual attitude toward selling bodily fluids for cash
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Bart's entrepreneurial amorality. Dark humor delivered with child-like enthusiasm.

jokes.text: Here you go, doll face. Okay, Homer. Just relax.
jokes.setup: Bart trying to pass as adult for blood donation
jokes.punchline: Nurse recognizing Homer, implying Bart used Homer's ID
jokes.explanation: Clever reveal - Bart stole Homer's ID for the scam, good visual comedy setup.

jokes.text: Homer was never stubborn. He always folded instantly. As if he had no will of his own. -Isn't that true, Homer? -Yes, Dad.
jokes.setup: Grampa comparing Bart's stubbornness to Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately proving the point by agreeing without thought
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup and instant payoff. Homer proves his father's point in real-time with zero self-awareness.

jokes.text: Twelve bucks and a free cookie. What a country.
jokes.setup: Bart's successful blood donation
jokes.punchline: Patriotic enthusiasm for capitalism at its most basic level
jokes.explanation: Nice touch of American optimism about the most mercenary transaction. Bart's enthusiasm is endearing.

jokes.text: Viva skid row! Hey, it's that anchor dude from Channel 6.
jokes.setup: Bart embracing his homeless situation
jokes.punchline: Cheerful celebration of being on skid row
jokes.explanation: Typical Bart resilience - finding excitement in dire circumstances.

jokes.text: We have lots of names for these people: Bums, deadbeats, losers, scums of the earth. We'd like to sweep them into the gutter or to some other out-of-the-way place.
jokes.setup: Reporter doing Thanksgiving story about helping the homeless
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest assessment of public attitudes toward homeless
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satirical journalism - saying the quiet part loud. Subverts typical feel-good Thanksgiving stories.

jokes.text: So every year on one lone, conscience-salving day... we toss these people a bone. A turkey bone.
jokes.setup: Reporter's cynical take on Thanksgiving charity
jokes.punchline: Literal 'turkey bone' pun for minimal charitable effort
jokes.explanation: Masterful wordplay - 'throw a bone' becomes literal 'turkey bone.' Satirical bite with perfect economy.

jokes.text: Pompous, blow-dried, college boy! -His girlfriend does the weather. -You don't say.
jokes.setup: Homeless men critiquing the reporter
jokes.punchline: Casual gossip about local TV personalities
jokes.explanation: Humanizes homeless characters through normal gossip. Shows they're still engaged with local culture.

jokes.text: How long have you been on the streets? Five years. Ha-ha! I didn't apologize!
jokes.setup: Reporter asking serious question about homelessness
jokes.punchline: Bart completely missing the gravity, celebrating his stubbornness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart - completely oblivious to the serious context, focused only on family conflict.

jokes.text: -Ha-ha! I didn't apologize! Give me the number for 911!
jokes.setup: Bart appearing on TV, Homer panicking
jokes.punchline: Homer's emergency response to non-emergency situation
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer panic response. Asking for 911 number shows his scatter-brained emergency thinking.

jokes.text: Give me the number for 911!
jokes.setup: Marge panicking about Bart being on TV
jokes.punchline: Not knowing the emergency number she's asking for
jokes.explanation: Good panic comedy - Marge's maternal panic making her forget obvious information.

jokes.text: Thanks for your help. This reporter smells another Emmy.
jokes.setup: Reporter finishing his cynical homeless story
jokes.punchline: Transparent self-promotion disguised as journalism
jokes.explanation: Good satire of exploitative journalism. Reporter's naked ambition perfectly captured.

jokes.text: Yeah, there's this family I hang out with. -Sounds pretty sweet. -Yeah, I guess it is.
jokes.setup: Reporter asking where Bart sleeps
jokes.punchline: Bart's realization mid-conversation that his family is actually good
jokes.explanation: Touching moment of Bart's dawning appreciation. Character growth delivered with perfect subtlety.

jokes.text: Guys, unless you feel weird about taking money... from a kid, I thought.... -I wouldn't feel weird. -I'm comfortable with it.
jokes.setup: Bart offering to share his money with homeless men
jokes.punchline: Their immediate moral flexibility about taking a child's money
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about survival versus morality. Their quick agreement is both funny and sad.

jokes.text: Let's go! If I'm not back at the home by 9... they declare me dead and collect my insurance.
jokes.setup: Grampa ready to leave dinner
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to nursing home insurance fraud
jokes.explanation: Perfect Grampa line - dark humor about elder care delivered matter-of-factly. Great character voice.

jokes.text: Let's go! If I'm not back at the home by 9, they declare me dead and collect my insurance.
jokes.setup: Family preparing to leave
jokes.punchline: Grandpa's matter-of-fact explanation of nursing home death procedures
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor - Grandpa's casual attitude about institutional death protocols is both funny and disturbing.

jokes.text: Get down on your knees and beg for forgiveness. Yeah, beg me, Bart. Beg me!
jokes.setup: Bart's return and attempt at simple apology
jokes.punchline: Family demanding increasingly dramatic gestures, Lisa getting carried away with power
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of family revenge. Lisa's sudden power trip is perfectly childlike.

jokes.text: Now we can blame him for everything! -It's your fault I'm bald! -Sorry. -It's your fault I'm old! -Sorry. -It's your fault I can't talk! -Sorry.
jokes.setup: Bart's complete capitulation to family
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous blame assignments including natural aging and laryngitis
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from reasonable blame to cosmic responsibility. The rhythm builds perfectly.

jokes.text: It's your fault America has lost its way. -It's all your fault. -I'm sorry! I'm sorry! I'm sorry!
jokes.setup: Blame escalating to ridiculous proportions
jokes.punchline: National decline blamed on 10-year-old, Bart's breakdown
jokes.explanation: Peak escalation - personal family drama becomes commentary on national malaise. Perfect absurdist climax.

jokes.text: Jackpot! A sellout crowd at the Super Bowl... Simpson down by six with two seconds left. He's got Simpson in the open. Touchdown, Simpson! The boy nobody wanted just won the Super Bowl!
jokes.setup: Bart playing alone with toys on the roof
jokes.punchline: Fantasy where he's the hero despite family rejection
jokes.explanation: Touching character moment showing Bart's need for validation through imagination. 'Boy nobody wanted' hits hard.

jokes.text: Jackpot! A sellout crowd at the Super Bowl... Simpson down by six with two seconds left. He's got Simpson in the open. Touchdown, Simpson! The boy nobody wanted just won the Super Bowl!
jokes.setup: Bart playing with toys alone on roof
jokes.punchline: Creating fantasy where he's the hero after being rejected
jokes.explanation: Good character psychology - Bart processing rejection through sports fantasy.

jokes.text: Because you hate me or because you're bad?
jokes.setup: Lisa asking why Bart destroyed her centerpiece
jokes.punchline: Child's binary thinking about motivation - personal or general badness
jokes.explanation: Perfect child psychology - reducing complex behavior to simple categories. Lisa's earnest question is touching.

jokes.text: I don't know. I don't know why I enjoyed it... or why I'll do it again!
jokes.setup: Lisa asking why he destroyed her centerpiece
jokes.punchline: Bart's honest self-assessment of his destructive nature
jokes.explanation: Excellent character insight - Bart's honest admission of his compulsive destructiveness.

jokes.text: I'm sorry, Lisa. -Apology accepted.
jokes.setup: Bart's long-awaited genuine apology after finding his empathy
jokes.punchline: Lisa's immediate, matter-of-fact acceptance
jokes.explanation: Touching resolution with perfect simplicity. Lisa's formal acceptance shows her maturity and forgiveness.
